qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default forwarded-header trust lets clients spoof backend security metadata
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is about Traefik v1.7 default entrypoint forwarding trusting client-supplied `X-Forwarded-*` headers on normal backend requests (`configuration/configuration.go`, `configuration/entrypoints.go`, `server/header_rewriter.go`, `server/server_configuration.go`). The advisory instead targets the distinct `ForwardAuth` auth-subrequest path where `pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go` fails to strip or rebuild attacker-controlled `X-Forwarded-Prefix` when `trustForwardHeader=false`, often after `StripPrefix`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cleartext h2c preface handling lets clients pin front-end connections indefinitely
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a cleartext h2c connection-stall denial of service in `h2c/h2c.go` and `server/server.go`. It does not involve `ForwardAuth`, auth-service subrequests, `X-Forwarded-Prefix`, `StripPrefix`, or an authentication bypass behind a trusted upstream proxy.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's `ForwardAuth` root cause: attacker-controlled `X-Forwarded-Prefix` surviving into the auth subrequest when `trustForwardHeader=false` behind a trusted upstream proxy.
evidence: The advisory describes `pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go` copying request headers into the auth subrequest and failing to strip or rebuild `X-Forwarded-Prefix`, with `StripPrefix` potentially appending a second value before the auth service evaluates the first one.

The closest session finding, `forwarded_header_default_trust`, is a different v1.7 bug on the normal backend forwarding path where default entrypoint config sets `ForwardedHeaders.Insecure=true`; it does not mention `ForwardAuth`, auth subrequests, `trustForwardHeader=false`, or `X-Forwarded-Prefix`.
`h2c_preface_stall_dos` is an unrelated availability issue in cleartext h2c preface handling.
